zsh.h(3) Library Functions Manual zsh.h(3)

NAME

zsh.h - Zsh shell completion generator.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


asciichat_error_t completions_generate_zsh (FILE *output)
Generate zsh completion script.

Detailed Description

Zsh shell completion generator.

Definition in file zsh.h.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t completions_generate_zsh (FILE * output)

Generate zsh completion script.

Definition at line 190 of file zsh.c.

190                                                          {
191   if (!output) {
192     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Output stream cannot be NULL");
193   }
194 
195   /* Load binary-level and discovery options for binary-level completion.
196    * Binary-level completion (options before mode name like --color-mode) uses
197    * _arguments with -n -S flags to establish proper zsh completion context.
198    * Without _arguments, completion functions fail with "can only be called from
199    * completion function" error, causing zsh to display "corrections (errors: N)"
200    * messages. The -n flag disables defaults and -S disables short option
201    * processing, allowing safe context establishment. */
202   size_t binary_count = 0;
203   const option_descriptor_t *binary_opts = options_registry_get_for_display(MODE_DISCOVERY, true, &binary_count);
204 
205   size_t discovery_count = 0;
206   const option_descriptor_t *discovery_opts = options_registry_get_for_display(MODE_DISCOVERY, false, &discovery_count);
207 
208   fprintf(output, "compdef _ascii_chat ascii-chat\n"
209                   "compdef _ascii_chat 'build/bin/ascii-chat'\n"
210                   "# Zsh completion script for ascii-chat\n"
211                   "# Generated from options registry - DO NOT EDIT MANUALLY\n"
212                   "\n"
213                   "\n"
214                   "# Configure completion for ascii-chat\n"
215                   "# Use only _complete (no corrections or approximate matching)\n"
216                   "zstyle ':completion:*:ascii-chat:*' completer _complete\n"
217                   "\n"
218                   "# Device index completion helpers\n"
219                   "_ascii_chat_webcam_indices() {\n"
220                   "  local -a indices\n"
221                   "  local output\n"
222                   "  output=$(\"${words[1]}\" --list-webcams 2>/dev/null) || return\n"
223                   "  while IFS= read -r line; do\n"
224                   "    [[ \"$line\" =~ ^[[:space:]]*([0-9]+)[[:space:]]+(.+)$ ]] && \\\n"
225                   "      indices+=(\"${match[1]}:${match[2]}\")\n"
226                   "  done <<< \"$output\"\n"
227                   "  _describe 'webcam' indices\n"
228                   "}\n"
229                   "\n"
230                   "_ascii_chat_microphone_indices() {\n"
231                   "  local -a indices\n"
232                   "  local output\n"
233                   "  output=$(\"${words[1]}\" --list-microphones 2>/dev/null) || return\n"
234                   "  while IFS= read -r line; do\n"
235                   "    [[ \"$line\" =~ ^[[:space:]]*([0-9-]+)[[:space:]]+(.+)$ ]] && \\\n"
236                   "      indices+=(\"${match[1]}:${match[2]}\")\n"
237                   "  done <<< \"$output\"\n"
238                   "  _describe 'microphone' indices\n"
239                   "}\n"
240                   "\n"
241                   "_ascii_chat_speakers_indices() {\n"
242                   "  local -a indices\n"
243                   "  local output\n"
244                   "  output=$(\"${words[1]}\" --list-speakers 2>/dev/null) || return\n"
245                   "  while IFS= read -r line; do\n"
246                   "    [[ \"$line\" =~ ^[[:space:]]*([0-9-]+)[[:space:]]+(.+)$ ]] && \\\n"
247                   "      indices+=(\"${match[1]}:${match[2]}\")\n"
248                   "  done <<< \"$output\"\n"
249                   "  _describe 'speakers' indices\n"
250                   "}\n"
251                   "\n"
252                   "_ascii_chat_binary() {\n"
253                   "  local -a binary_opts=(\n");
254 
255   /* Add discovery options to binary-level completion */
256   if (discovery_opts) {
257     for (size_t i = 0; i < discovery_count; i++) {
258       fprintf(output, "    '--%s:", discovery_opts[i].long_name);
259       zsh_escape_desc(output, discovery_opts[i].help_text);
260       fprintf(output, "'\n");
261     }
262   }
263 
264   fprintf(output, "  )\n"
265                   "  _values 'option' \"${binary_opts[@]}\"\n"
266                   "}\n"
267                   "\n"
268                   "_ascii_chat_binary_grouped() {\n");
269 
270   /* Write binary-level options in grouped format */
271   if (binary_opts) {
272     zsh_write_value_cases(output, binary_opts, binary_count);
273     zsh_write_options_grouped(output, binary_opts, binary_count, "binary");
274   }
275 
276   fprintf(output, "}\n"
277                   "\n"
278                   "_ascii_chat() {\n"
279                   "  if [[ ${words[2]} == -* ]]; then\n"
280                   "    # Binary-level options: show both binary and discovery mode options\n"
281                   "    # (since discovery is the default mode that runs at binary level)\n"
282                   "    _ascii_chat_binary_grouped\n"
283                   "    _ascii_chat_discovery\n"
284                   "    return\n"
285                   "  fi\n"
286                   "\n"
287                   "  # Mode routing: check words[2] to determine which mode function to call\n"
288                   "  case $words[2] in\n"
289                   "    # Server-like modes\n"
290                   "    server)\n"
291                   "      _ascii_chat_server\n"
292                   "      return\n"
293                   "      ;;\n"
294                   "    discovery-service)\n"
295                   "      _ascii_chat_discovery_service\n"
296                   "      return\n"
297                   "      ;;\n"
298                   "\n"
299                   "    # Client-like modes\n"
300                   "    client)\n"
301                   "      _ascii_chat_client\n"
302                   "      return\n"
303                   "      ;;\n"
304                   "    mirror)\n"
305                   "      _ascii_chat_mirror\n"
306                   "      return\n"
307                   "      ;;\n"
308                   "    # Default/unknown input - show mode selection only if truly empty, otherwise discovery\n"
309                   "    *)\n"
310                   "      # If words[2] is empty AND we're at position 2, show mode selection\n"
311                   "      # Otherwise (even for partial input like 'disc'), use discovery mode (the default)\n"
312                   "      if [[ -z \"${words[2]}\" && $CURRENT -eq 2 ]]; then\n"
313                   "        # Completing the mode name itself - show available modes\n"
314                   "        local -a server_modes client_modes\n"
315                   "        server_modes=(\n");
316 
317   /* Generate server-like modes from registry */
318   size_t mode_server_count = 0;
319   const mode_descriptor_t *mode_server_descs = get_modes_by_group("server-like", &mode_server_count);
320   if (mode_server_descs) {
321     for (size_t i = 0; i < mode_server_count; i++) {
322       fprintf(output, "          '%s:%s'\n", mode_server_descs[i].name, mode_server_descs[i].description);
323     }
324     SAFE_FREE(mode_server_descs);
325   }
326 
327   fprintf(output, "        )\n"
328                   "        client_modes=(\n");
329 
330   /* Generate client-like modes from registry (exclude discovery since it's the default mode) */
331   size_t mode_client_count = 0;
332   const mode_descriptor_t *mode_client_descs = get_modes_by_group("client-like", &mode_client_count);
333   if (mode_client_descs) {
334     for (size_t i = 0; i < mode_client_count; i++) {
335       /* Skip discovery mode - it's the default when no mode is specified */
336       if (strcmp(mode_client_descs[i].name, "discovery") == 0) {
337         continue;
338       }
339       fprintf(output, "          '%s:%s'\n", mode_client_descs[i].name, mode_client_descs[i].description);
340     }
341     SAFE_FREE(mode_client_descs);
342   }
343 
344   fprintf(output, "        )\n"
345                   "        _describe -t server-modes 'server-like modes' server_modes\n"
346                   "        _describe -t client-modes 'client-like modes' client_modes\n"
347                   "      else\n"
348                   "        # Default mode (discovery) - complete its options\n"
349                   "        _ascii_chat_discovery\n"
350                   "      fi\n"
351                   "      ;;\n"
352                   "  esac\n"
353                   "}\n"
354                   "\n"
355                   "# Server-like modes: handle incoming connections and stream management\n"
356                   "_ascii_chat_server() {\n");
357 
358   /* Server options - grouped by category */
359   size_t server_count = 0;
360   const option_descriptor_t *server_opts = options_registry_get_for_display(MODE_SERVER, false, &server_count);
361 
362   if (server_opts) {
363     zsh_write_value_cases(output, server_opts, server_count);
364     zsh_write_options_grouped(output, server_opts, server_count, "server");
365     SAFE_FREE(server_opts);
366   }
367 
368   fprintf(output, "}\n"
369                   "\n"
370                   "_ascii_chat_discovery_service() {\n");
371 
372   /* Discovery-service options - grouped by category */
373   size_t discovery_svc_count = 0;
374   const option_descriptor_t *discovery_svc_opts =
375       options_registry_get_for_display(MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE, false, &discovery_svc_count);
376 
377   if (discovery_svc_opts) {
378     zsh_write_value_cases(output, discovery_svc_opts, discovery_svc_count);
379     zsh_write_options_grouped(output, discovery_svc_opts, discovery_svc_count, "discovery_service");
380     SAFE_FREE(discovery_svc_opts);
381   }
382 
383   fprintf(output, "}\n"
384                   "\n"
385                   "# Client-like modes: connect to servers or render local media\n"
386                   "_ascii_chat_client() {\n");
387 
388   /* Client options - grouped by category */
389   size_t client_count = 0;
390   const option_descriptor_t *client_opts = options_registry_get_for_display(MODE_CLIENT, false, &client_count);
391 
392   if (client_opts) {
393     zsh_write_value_cases(output, client_opts, client_count);
394     zsh_write_options_grouped(output, client_opts, client_count, "client");
395     SAFE_FREE(client_opts);
396   }
397 
398   fprintf(output, "}\n"
399                   "\n"
400                   "_ascii_chat_mirror() {\n");
401 
402   /* Mirror options - grouped by category */
403   size_t mirror_count = 0;
404   const option_descriptor_t *mirror_opts = options_registry_get_for_display(MODE_MIRROR, false, &mirror_count);
405 
406   if (mirror_opts) {
407     zsh_write_value_cases(output, mirror_opts, mirror_count);
408     zsh_write_options_grouped(output, mirror_opts, mirror_count, "mirror");
409     SAFE_FREE(mirror_opts);
410   }
411 
412   fprintf(output, "}\n"
413                   "\n"
414                   "# Discovery mode: find sessions via discovery service (default mode when no mode specified)\n"
415                   "_ascii_chat_discovery() {\n");
416 
417   /* Discovery options - grouped by category (reuse already-loaded options) */
418   if (discovery_opts) {
419     zsh_write_value_cases(output, discovery_opts, discovery_count);
420     zsh_write_options_grouped(output, discovery_opts, discovery_count, "discovery");
421   }
422 
423   fprintf(output, "}\n"
424                   "\n"
425                   "_ascii_chat \"$@\"\n");
426 
427   SAFE_FREE(binary_opts);
428   SAFE_FREE(discovery_opts);
429 
430   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
431 }










Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>







  

    Version 0.9.3
    ascii-chat
